goal: Find one or more new orthogonal specialist signals or conditioning layers that work where active-signal-v1@1 is absent, prove their additive value, and avoid repeating v1's single-filter event-entry mistakes.
global_constraints: Base active-signal-v1@1 is immutable: no retuning of hours, days, thresholds, or execution rules.
Raw single-filter event-entry constructions (H1/H2 style) are permanently banned.
Every new candidate must first pass complement-first mode before any other evaluation.
Model-first approaches are forbidden until the low-friction Wave A is fully exhausted.
Each prototype must have at least three logical layers: new information layer, regime/state confirmation, and entry-density limiter.
A candidate whose profit vanishes under strict-additive mode is rejected regardless of permissive-integrated performance.

slices.title: Complement-first standalone backtests
slices.hypothesis: Verify whether each prototype has standalone value specifically on the base-absent surface before any integration testing.
slices.objective: Verify whether each prototype has standalone value specifically on the base-absent surface before any integration testing.
success_criteria: A shortlist of prototypes exists that look like real specialist candidates even in complement-only mode.
evidence_requirements: A shortlist of prototypes exists that look like real specialist candidates even in complement-only mode.

slices.title: Stability, concentration and specialist verification
slices.hypothesis: Retain only prototypes that work as genuine specialist layers across time, not those riding a single lucky historical stretch.
slices.objective: Retain only prototypes that work as genuine specialist layers across time, not those riding a single lucky historical stretch.
success_criteria: Only stable specialists or honestly limited but reproducible regime-repair layers remain.
evidence_requirements: Only stable specialists or honestly limited but reproducible regime-repair layers remain.

slices.title: Integration with base in two modes
slices.hypothesis: Test every finalist in both strict-additive and permissive-integrated modes against the unchanged base to separate genuinely additive signals from those that look good only by mixing with base trades.
slices.objective: Test every finalist in both strict-additive and permissive-integrated modes against the unchanged base to separate genuinely additive signals from those that look good only by mixing with base trades.
success_criteria: A clear picture exists of who is genuinely additive versus who only looks good by mixing with the base.
evidence_requirements: A clear picture exists of who is genuinely additive versus who only looks good by mixing with the base.
